instruction: Normalize W1/W2/W3 into comparable dimensionless participation values before building the global shell.

problem.name: unit dominance
problem.symptom: W3 frequency dominates the global shell because its numeric scale is much larger than W1 amplitude and W2 phase.
problem.effect: channel ratios measure unit scale instead of relational participation.

core_rule.after: equalize each domain first, then build global_shell
core_rule.principle: channels should report ratios of relation, not raw units.

code_changes.name: equalize_domains
code_changes.code:
def equalize_domains(w_delta, eps=1e-12):
    """
    Convert W1/W2/W3 deltas into comparable dimensionless participation values.
    This prevents frequency-scale dominance over amplitude/phase.
    """
    W = np.abs(np.array(w_delta, dtype=np.float64))

    # Local frame-wise compression: preserves ordering but limits unit dominance.
    W = np.log1p(W)

    # Normalize by max so all domains live in [0, 1].
    max_w = float(np.max(W) + eps)
    W_eq = W / max_w

    return W_eq

code_changes.name: normalize_to_global_shell
code_changes.code:
def normalize_to_global_shell(w_delta, eps=1e-12):
    W_eq = equalize_domains(w_delta, eps=eps)

    total = float(np.sum(W_eq) + eps)
    shell = W_eq / total
    shell_magnitude = float(np.linalg.norm(W_eq))

    return shell, shell_magnitude

details: Do not compute shell from raw W1/W2/W3 units.

code_changes.implementation: Inside compute_channel_ratios, add 'domain_equalized_values': W_eq.tolist() by returning W_eq from normalize_to_global_shell or recomputing it once.

preferred_replace_full_functions.equalize_domains:
def equalize_domains(w_delta, eps=1e-12):
    W = np.abs(np.array(w_delta, dtype=np.float64))
    W = np.log1p(W)
    max_w = float(np.max(W) + eps)
    return W / max_w
preferred_replace_full_functions.normalize_to_global_shell:
def normalize_to_global_shell(w_delta, eps=1e-12):
    W_eq = equalize_domains(w_delta, eps=eps)
    total = float(np.sum(W_eq) + eps)
    shell = W_eq / total
    shell_magnitude = float(np.linalg.norm(W_eq))
    return shell, shell_magnitude, W_eq
preferred_replace_full_functions.compute_channel_ratios:
def compute_channel_ratios(w_delta, eps=1e-12):
    shell, shell_magnitude, W_eq = normalize_to_global_shell(w_delta, eps=eps)
    W1, W2, W3 = shell

    raw = np.array([
        W1,
        W2,
        W3,
        min(W1, W2),
        min(W1, W3),
        min(W2, W3),
        min(W1, W2, W3)
    ], dtype=np.float64)

    whole = float(np.sum(raw) + eps)
    ratios = raw / whole

    ch7_presence = 1.0 if np.all(shell > eps) else 0.0
    ch7_intensity = float(np.min(shell) / (np.max(shell) + eps))

    return {
        'whole': whole,
        'shell_magnitude': shell_magnitude,
        'domain_equalized_values': W_eq.tolist(),
        'global_shell': shell.tolist(),
        'raw_channels': raw.tolist(),
        'channel_ratios': ratios.tolist(),
        'ch7_presence': ch7_presence,
        'ch7_intensity': ch7_intensity
    }

plot_updates.title: Domain Equalized W1/W2/W3 Values
plot_updates.purpose: confirm frequency is no longer overwhelming the other domains

plot_updates.title: Global Shell W1/W2/W3 Participation
plot_updates.expected_after_patch: W3 should no longer sit at ~0.9 constantly unless the signal is truly frequency-dominant after equalization.

plot_updates.title: ch7 White Coupling Intensity
plot_updates.expected_after_patch: ch7 should become visibly nonzero when all three domains participate.

success_conditions: global shell no longer permanently dominated by W3
W1/W2/W3 all show visible participation when active
ch7 intensity is no longer collapsed to near zero purely because of unit scale
channel ratios still sum to approximately 1.0
validation.warning: If W2 remains tiny after equalization, the issue is not scale but the phase metric itself.

next_patch_after_success.name: axis_projection_8way_v1
next_patch_after_success.purpose: Add diagonal direction measurement after equalized global shell is stable.
